title: asperula oppositifolia subsp. oppositifolia
intro: Asperula oppositifolia subsp. oppositifolia, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Wood Sorrel include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting the immune system. Wood Sorrel can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and natural treatment option for various health concerns.
